name: MetroSelect
summary: Windows-8-Metro-style grid of flat, sharp-cornered tiles (`MetroSelect.Option`) for single or `multiple` selection; controlled by `value` + `onValueChange`. Tiles snap into a tight grid with a small default gutter, tilt in 3D on press, and accept a per-option `color` + `wide` 2×1 module — use it when options benefit from icons/imagery rather than a compact dropdown.

props.name: gap
props.description: Inter-tile spacing as units or CSS length.

props.name: size
props.description: Visual size of the tiles; token or explicit CSS size.

props.name: name
props.description:
Field identifier used for FormControl binding and forwarded to the underlying control
when applicable. Components can require `name` locally if they depend on it.

props.name: label
props.description: Visual label rendered by the component or its wrapper. Accepts string or node.

props.name: helperText
props.description: Supplemental hint or validation content typically rendered under the control.

props.name: error
props.description: Marks the field invalid; components may adjust visuals and helper text color.

props.name: fullWidth
props.description: Stretches the field wrapper to occupy the full available width.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

props.name: compact
props.description:
Zeros all layout spacing (pad, gap, and spacing-margins) where supported and
cascades to spacing-aware descendants. Does not touch control insets,
structural geometry, border-radius, glyph sizes, or alignment. `compact={false}`
opts a subtree back out of an inherited compact.

bestPractices: Use for compact choice sets where visual labels and icons help recognition.
Choose `multiple` when users should select more than one; otherwise keep single‑select.
Keep options short and distinct; avoid long labels that wrap unpredictably.
Expose clear values via `value` so form binding and analytics stay consistent.

Tiles snap into a tight Metro grid by default (≈8px gutter, 4px compact); pass `gap` only to override. They render as flat sharp-cornered filled blocks — give a tile a per-option `color` for the saturated Start-screen mosaic, and `wide` for a 2×1 module. A subtle 3D press-tilt (reduced-motion-gated) reinforces the Metro feel; selection adds an inset keyline so it isn't colour-only.
Keyboard: ensure arrow keys move focus predictably and Space toggles selection.

Binding precedence (prop > form > internal). An explicit `value` prop always wins; otherwise a `name` inside a `FormControl` binds to the store (seed the key in `createFormStore(initial)` so the initial selection is honoured); otherwise the control is uncontrolled. The binding is latched at mount.
Change source. `onValueChange`/`onValueCommit` report a truthful `info.source`: `'pointer'` for a tile click and `'keyboard'` for keyboard activation (Enter/Space), not a blanket `'programmatic'`.

Touch + form config. Each tile is a tap target floored at >=44px on coarse pointers (40px under `compact`) with the mobile chrome kit (no blue tap-flash); inside a `FormControl`, the field-level `disabled`/`error` merge with the form-wide config (own props win) while a per-tile `disabled` stays independent.

examples.title: Uncontrolled
examples.code:
<MetroSelect defaultValue='home'>
  <MetroSelect.Option icon='mdi:home' value='home' label='Home' />
  <MetroSelect.Option icon='mdi:briefcase' value='work' label='Work' disabled />
  <MetroSelect.Option icon='mdi:airplane' value='travel' label='Travel' />
</MetroSelect>

examples.title: Controlled value
Multiple selection
examples.code:
<MetroSelect multiple defaultValue={['home','travel']}>
  <MetroSelect.Option icon='mdi:home' value='home' label='Home' />
  <MetroSelect.Option icon='mdi:airplane' value='travel' label='Travel' />
  <MetroSelect.Option icon='mdi:briefcase' value='work' label='Work' disabled />
</MetroSelect>

examples.title: Metro mosaic (color + wide)
examples.code:
<MetroSelect defaultValue='mail'>
  <MetroSelect.Option icon='mdi:email' value='mail' label='Mail' color='#0078d7' wide />
  <MetroSelect.Option icon='mdi:image' value='photos' label='Photos' color='#107c10' />
  <MetroSelect.Option icon='mdi:music' value='music' label='Music' color='#e3008c' />
  <MetroSelect.Option icon='mdi:store' value='store' label='Store' color='#5c2d91' />
</MetroSelect>
